topics.title: Open Neuroimaging Workflows and Data Standards
topics.description: This cluster centers on open, standardized neuroimaging research workflows, including data conversion, dataset organization, and automated processing. It also reflects clinical and collaborative efforts around visualization, annotation, and reproducible analysis.

keywords: functional mri compatible cortical wide field fluorescent
topics.title: Integrated Optical and fMRI Brain Mapping
topics.description: This cluster centers on combining optical neuroimaging, calcium imaging, and fMRI to study brain activity and connectivity across scales. The abstracts emphasize multimodal mapping approaches for networks, hemodynamics, and concurrent imaging in awake mice.

topics.title: MEG markers of Alzheimer-related aging
topics.description: This cluster centers on Alzheimer disease and tau related neurodegeneration across the lifespan, with emphasis on age related changes in brain anatomy and size. It also highlights MEG-based functional markers such as rest state MEG activity, spectral power, neural oscillation, and frequency specific neural synchrony deficits.

keywords: the trigeminal nerve in classical trigeminal neuralgia
topics.title: Neural Signatures in Tinnitus and Trigeminal Neuralgia
topics.description: This cluster centers on structural and microstructural brain differences associated with tinnitus and trigeminal neuralgia, including shared brain signatures and alterations in the trigeminal nerve, claustrum, and prefrontal cortex. It also highlights abnormalities related to phantom sound perception and barrier permeability dynamics in affected patients.

topics.title: Connectomic DBS and Circuit-Based Targeting
topics.description: This cluster centers on deep brain stimulation studied through connectomic and functional network approaches to identify causal circuits, targets, and programming strategies. The abstracts emphasize network-based localization, white matter substrates, and symptom or outcome prediction across DBS applications.

keywords: functional mapping of cerebellothalamic networks after focused ultrasound
topics.title: Focused Ultrasound Thalamotomy in Tremor
topics.description: This cluster centers on focused ultrasound thalamotomy for essential tremor, with emphasis on Vim targeting, symptom and motor improvement, and durable clinical outcomes. It also highlights connectivity-based and functional mapping approaches to understand cerebellothalamic network reorganization and predict treatment response.

keywords: assessment of neural correlates of language processing in multilingual speakers
topics.title: fNIRS Studies of Language Processing
topics.description: This cluster centers on using fNIRS to examine neural correlates of language processing across multilingual and bilingual populations, including aphasia and language proficiency. It also includes related language tasks and regional brain activity during rest and speech-related processing.

keywords: functional network connectivity patterns across cognitive phenotypes
topics.title: Cognitive decline and network connectivity changes
topics.description: The cluster centers on cognitive decline, mild cognitive impairment, and related neurological conditions such as Alzheimer disease, Parkinson disease, and delirium. It also highlights altered functional and dynamic network connectivity patterns across cognitive phenotypes.

keywords: environment shaped pregnancy vulnerable brain patterns predict maternal child mental health
prenatal maternal mood modulates longitudinal trajectories
prenatal maternal mood modulates longitudinal trajectories of brain development
topics.title: Maternal Mood and Early Brain Development
topics.description: These studies examine how prenatal and perinatal maternal mood relate to fetal, neonatal, and childhood brain development and connectivity. The cluster also links maternal depression and related exposures to later mental health outcomes and neurodevelopmental risk.

keywords: gradient organization of cortical metabolic connectivity
functional and metabolic network signatures of
topics.title: Cortical Metabolic Connectomics in Neurodegeneration
topics.description: This cluster centers on brain MRSI-based connectomics and metabolic gradient organization in the human brain, linking cortical metabolic connectivity with network signatures and neurometabolic foundations. The abstracts emphasize individualized, multimodal mapping and applications to disorders such as multiple sclerosis and alzheimer disease.

topics.title: Glutamatergic Neurochemistry in Brain Disorders
topics.description: This cluster centers on glutamatergic and neurometabolic alterations measured in brain regions such as the prefrontal cortex and striatum across conditions including tinnitus, depression, and Parkinson disease. It emphasizes metabolite changes and biomarkers linked to stress, treatment, and disorder-specific neurochemistry.

topics.title: Multisite Neuroimaging Harmonization and Modeling
topics.description: This cluster centers on methods for correcting site effects and evaluating harmonization in multisite neuroimaging data. It also includes predictive and normative modeling tools for reproducible analysis across heterogeneous datasets.

topics.title: Olfactory Processing and Dysfunction
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how smell and body odor are processed in the brain, including odor-induced activation, self-body odor comparison, and mechanisms underlying olfactory identification. It also includes studies of olfactory dysfunction and congenital anosmia in patients, with links to experience-dependent modulation and fear responses.

topics.title: Lewy Body and Parkinsonian Disease Progression
topics.description: This cluster centers on Parkinson disease and related Lewy body disorders, especially early and prodromal stages, phenoconversion, and associated clinical features such as cognitive decline and visual hallucinations. It also highlights underlying neurobiological changes including substantia nigra involvement, alpha-synuclein pathology, midbrain connectivity, and brainstem/cholinergic abnormalities.

topics.title: Midlife menopause and hippocampal memory changes
topics.description: This cluster centers on how the menopause transition and midlife relate to hippocampal subfield structure and memory outcomes. It also includes links to subjective cognitive decline, navigation performance, and cortical microstructure.

keywords: time vary scale free brain dynamic
topics.title: Naturalistic fMRI and Neural Dynamics
topics.description: This cluster centers on how naturalistic stimuli and movie viewing reveal temporal dynamics in neural representations and variability. It includes work on neural typicality, long-term dynamics, and scale-free or multifractal brain activity during fMRI.

topics.title: Multimodal Meta-Analytic Brain Organization
topics.description: This cluster centers on meta-analytic and multimodal neuroimaging approaches used to map brain organization, connectivity, and gradients across psychiatric and neurological conditions. It also includes neurochemical and receptor-architecture themes that relate structural and functional patterns to clinical phenomena.

topics.title: Chronic Pain Networks and Phenotypes
topics.description: This cluster centers on how chronic pain is represented across brain and spinal cord systems, including pain perception, functional connectivity, and cortical or spinal phenotypes. It also includes longitudinal and condition-specific work on pain trajectories, sensitization, and fear-avoidance processes.

keywords: causal contribution of primary motor cortex
topics.title: Brain Stimulation and Motor Function Studies
topics.description: This cluster centers on noninvasive brain stimulation techniques and their use in probing or modulating motor and cognitive function, especially in stroke and related neurophysiological contexts. It also includes work on mapping, connectivity, and causal brain contributions using TMS, tUS, tDCS, and combined imaging approaches.

keywords: the neural correlates of hand gesture planning and execution
representational organization of hand actions across motor execution
motor learning through imitation of fully visible
topics.title: Action Observation and Sensorimotor Representation
topics.description: This cluster centers on how observing, imitating, and executing hand actions shape sensorimotor representations in the brain. It emphasizes action observation networks, hand and finger maps, and neural reorganization related to motor learning and grasping.

keywords: deep learning accelerated reconstruction of self
deep learning denoise for single excitation wideband
topics.title: Deep Learning MRI Analysis for Epilepsy
topics.description: The cluster centers on deep learning methods for MRI analysis, especially automated segmentation, reconstruction, and related pipeline improvements. Many abstracts focus on epilepsy and epileptogenic malformations, using brain MRI to support lesion detection and clinical analysis.

keywords: the effect of resolution on hippocampus segmentation
topics.title: MRI Methods for Hippocampal Segmentation
topics.description: This cluster centers on MRI acquisition and processing methods for hippocampus and hippocampal subfield segmentation across different field strengths and resolutions. It includes reconstruction, registration, and quantitative approaches aimed at improving morphometric accuracy and delineation.

keywords: an automatic pipeline for sulcal feature extraction
topics.title: Cortical Folding Atlas Construction and Segmentation
topics.description: This cluster centers on building and analyzing cortical folding atlases and related brain shape representations, often using high-resolution datasets such as BigBrain. It also includes automated segmentation, registration, and pipeline-based methods for reconstructing and parcellating brain anatomy.

topics.title: Trauma-Related Network Dysfunction and Emotion Regulation
topics.description: This cluster centers on how childhood and adulthood trauma, including intimate partner violence and early life stress, relates to brain network dysfunction and altered emotion regulation. Studies link these experiences to resting-state and state connectivity, functional network signatures, and neurotransmitter profiles across PTSD and major depressive disorder.

keywords: constrained dynamic functional connectivity predicts surgical outcome
constrained dynamic functional connectivity predicts surgical outcome of temporal lobe epilepsy
the functional structural reorganization of connector hubs
topics.title: Network Biomarkers in Temporal Lobe Epilepsy
topics.description: This cluster centers on temporal lobe and focal epilepsy, emphasizing network dysfunction, altered dynamic functional connectivity, and connector hub reorganization. It also highlights presurgical evaluation and predictors of surgical outcome and seizure control.

keywords: the motion sick brain spectral changes
real time fmri trigger experience sampling
topics.title: Motion Sickness Brain Signals and Neurofeedback
topics.description: This cluster centers on neural and physiological signatures of motion sickness, including susceptibility, spectral changes, and regional brain activation during experimental induction. It also includes neurofeedback and real-time fMRI/EEG approaches aimed at self-regulation and related applications.

keywords: local correlation of brain function in youth onset
topics.title: Functional Connectivity Alterations Across Neurological Conditions
topics.description: This cluster centers on changes in functional connectivity and local correlation measured with resting-state fMRI and related network analyses. The studies examine how brain network alterations act as markers across diverse neurological disorders and injury states.

topics.title: Reproducible Multi-Echo fMRI Analysis Methods
topics.description: This cluster centers on methods for improving fMRI data quality, reproducibility, and bias control, especially in multi-echo and denoising workflows. It also includes modeling approaches for neuroimaging variability and spatial effects in functional data.

keywords: functional network remapping following brain tumor surgery
brain network and microenvironment shape glioma susceptible areas and modulate tumor progression
topics.title: Brain Network Changes in Glioma Surgery
topics.description: This cluster centers on glioma and glioblastoma research examining how tumors and surgery alter brain networks, connectivity, and functional remapping. It also includes efforts to predict postoperative deficits, functional recovery, survival, and therapy response using presurgical and network-based neuroimaging measures.

keywords: map organizational gradients of cortical growth use spectral normative models
topics.title: Normative Models of Cortical Development
topics.description: This cluster focuses on using normative modelling and spectral normative models to characterize cortical growth, thickness, and structural variation in the adult human brain. The studies examine altered cortical architecture and regional gradients across cortical and subcortical measures.

topics.title: Neuromodulation and Depression Treatment Response
topics.description: This cluster centers on depression and treatment-resistant depression, with emphasis on neuromodulation approaches such as tms, electroconvulsive therapy, and stanford neuromodulation therapy. It also highlights brain circuits and predictors of clinical and antidepressant treatment response.

topics.title: Autism Connectivity and Developmental Subgroups
topics.description: This cluster centers on autism spectrum disorder across childhood and adolescence, emphasizing clinically distinct and heterogeneous subgroups. The abstracts focus on altered functional connectivity, brain network characteristics, and developmental maturation in autism.

keywords: brainsymphony reveal psilocybin induced network reorganization
individualized parcellation reveal dorsal attention network specificity
topics.title: Psilocybin Effects on Brain Network Organization
topics.description: This cluster centers on how psilocybin alters brain connectivity and large-scale network organization, including default and action-coupling disruptions. It also highlights individualized parcellation and higher-order connectivity analyses used to reveal network reorganization and dorsal attention network specificity.

keywords: atlas guided region wise shapley value
topics.title: Explainable MRI Deep Learning for Dementia
topics.description: This cluster centers on applying deep learning and explainable AI to MRI for dementia and Alzheimer’s-related diagnosis, progression, and neuropathology prediction. It emphasizes atlas-guided interpretation, rationale generation, and model analysis for clinically interpretable brain imaging.

keywords: the impact of bioenergetic challenges on functional brain dynamics
topics.title: Bioenergetic Challenges and Brain Dynamics
topics.description: This cluster centers on how bioenergetic challenges, feeding state, and related interventions influence functional brain dynamics and connectivity. The abstracts link these effects to neural response, cognitive function, reaction time, and recovery in human fMRI studies.

keywords: network level connectivity alterations associate with suicide attempts
association of hippocampal subfield volumes with
social emotion recognition and suicide risk in psychiatric disorders a multimodal mri study
topics.title: MRI markers of suicide risk in depression
topics.description: This cluster centers on neuroimaging studies of suicide risk, suicidal ideation, and suicide attempts in depressive and other psychiatric disorders. It highlights network connectivity, amygdala functional connectivity, hippocampal subfield volumes, and social emotion recognition as potential biomarkers.

topics.title: Brain Aging, Cognition, and Predictors
topics.description: This cluster centers on brain aging across the lifespan, linking structural and multimodal imaging findings with cognition, development, and atrophy. It also includes predictive and biological factors such as genetics, parental longevity, and epigenetic age signatures, with attention to healthy older adults and Latin American cohorts.

keywords: convolutional neural network for brain age prediction
deep learning base brain age prediction
brain age gap reduction following exercise mirrors clinical improvements
functional connectivity base brain age gap
topics.title: Brain Age Prediction and Aging Models
topics.description: This cluster centers on MRI-based brain age estimation and prediction models used to characterize brain aging and related clinical change. It also includes work on model generalization, brain age gaps, and applications for detecting disease or tracking intervention effects.

topics.title: Neural Predictors of OCD Treatment Response
topics.description: This cluster centers on obsessive compulsive disorder, its cognitive and circuit-level features, and how these relate to treatment effects. Studies emphasize frontostriatal and executive network activity, resting-state connectivity, and neural predictors of response to CBT and other interventions.

keywords: functional connectivity predict crave across substance use disorder
topics.title: Cue Reactivity and Connectivity in Addiction
topics.description: This cluster centers on neural circuitry underlying craving and substance use disorders, especially responses to alcohol and drug cues. It emphasizes reward-related regions and functional connectivity patterns linked to craving, cue reactivity, and addiction phenotypes.

keywords: neural correlates of optimal risk taking
topics.title: Neural and Computational Bases of Risky Decision-Making
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how decision making under risk is shaped by reward learning, belief updating, and value computation. It also highlights neural and dopaminergic mechanisms underlying optimal risk taking and related individual differences.

keywords: semantic information in environmental sound categories
topics.title: Deafness, Sign Language, and Cortical Plasticity
topics.description: This cluster centers on how deafness and sign language experience shape neural organization and perception across visual and auditory-related systems. It includes work on visual speech, environmental sound semantics, and functional gradients in human brain function.

topics.title: Oscillatory Dynamics in Memory and Agency
topics.description: This cluster centers on neural oscillations and alpha-band activity in relation to memory, imagination, and agency-related processing. It links oscillatory mechanisms and functional connectivity with episodic, autobiographical, and working memory tasks.

topics.title: MRI and Biomarkers in Cognitive Aging
topics.description: This cluster centers on Alzheimer's-related cognitive decline and aging, highlighting MRI-based markers, white matter changes, and biomarker associations. It also includes inflammation-linked and multisite imaging approaches for characterizing disease burden and brain structure.

keywords: accelerate zero echo time functional mri
topics.title: Advanced fMRI Methods and Connectivity
topics.description: This cluster centers on advanced fMRI acquisition, denoising, and correction methods, especially for high-field and multi-echo imaging. It also includes analyses of intrinsic and task-based functional connectivity and network organization.

keywords: meta learning improve cross site depression severity prediction
topics.title: Cross-site fMRI biomarkers for psychiatric prediction
topics.description: This cluster centers on predicting and classifying psychiatric conditions and symptom severity from resting-state and multimodal MRI, with emphasis on depression, schizophrenia, bipolar disorder, and early psychosis. It also highlights challenges and methods for harmonization, transdiagnostic biotypes, and machine learning across sites.

keywords: multivariate bayesian inversion for trial wise decoding
topics.title: Dynamic Brain States in fMRI and MEG
topics.description: This cluster centers on modeling time-varying brain states and functional connectivity in fMRI and MEG. It emphasizes hidden-state dynamics, trial-wise decoding, and individual-level neural fingerprints across task and resting-state data.

keywords: demonstrate human task base bold fmri
topics.title: Advanced Human fMRI Measurement Methods
topics.description: This cluster centers on advanced MRI and multimodal acquisition approaches for studying human brain activity. It emphasizes high-resolution, high-temporal-resolution, and concurrent EEG/fMRI or stimulation-based methods for improving measurement and interpretation of BOLD and related signals.

keywords: psilocybin modulates functional brain connectivity differently
psychedelic desynchronize specific human brain network
topics.title: Psychedelics and Brain Connectivity Dynamics
topics.description: This cluster centers on how psilocybin and related compounds alter human brain network dynamics and functional connectivity, including receptor-linked mechanisms and dynamic causal modeling. It also includes work on modafinil-driven connectivity changes and molecular-imaging frameworks for linking brain networks to gene maps.

keywords: state connectivity task base impulsivity measure
topics.title: Connectivity and Fear Circuitry in OCD
topics.description: This cluster centers on brain connectivity, especially amygdala-frontal and default mode network alterations, in relation to fear learning, inhibitory control, and OCD. It also includes work on acute state effects, rest, and related functional measures in healthy adults and experimental challenges.

topics.title: Naturalistic Social Cognition and Empathy
topics.description: This cluster centers on social cognition in naturalistic and evaluative contexts, including empathy, perspective taking, social comparison, and social decision making. It also links these processes to clinical and trait differences such as psychopathic traits, fibromyalgia, depression, and behavioral variant frontotemporal dementia.

topics.title: Genetic Architecture of Brain and Psychiatric Traits
topics.description: This cluster centers on how genetic variation relates to brain structure, connectivity, and neuropsychiatric phenotypes such as depression and broader psychopathology. It emphasizes genetically informed analyses linking brain organization to psychiatric risk and symptom dimensions.

keywords: neurocomputational mechanisms of self esteem updating during social evaluation in adolescence
estimation of uncertainty of socio affective stimuli
topics.title: Adolescent social evaluation and uncertainty
topics.description: This cluster centers on how adolescents update self-esteem during social evaluation, especially under uncertainty, threat, and affective stimulus processing. It also links these processes to stress-related neural mechanisms, including reward prediction error signaling and ventral tegmental area responses measured with fMRI.

topics.title: Brain Development Across Populations and Lifespan
topics.description: This cluster centers on how brain structure and maturation vary from prematurity through infancy and across the human lifespan, with emphasis on population-specific developmental trajectories and normative brain charts. It also includes work on structural and network-level brain measures and their modeling in clinical MRI cohorts.

topics.title: Adolescent Neurodevelopment and Psychopathology
topics.description: This cluster centers on adolescent brain development in relation to adversity, resilience, and psychopathology. It emphasizes longitudinal links between neurodevelopmental changes and behavioral or mental health outcomes across youth.

keywords: bold asl base cerebrovascular reactivity mapping
topics.title: Cerebrovascular Reactivity and Perfusion Mapping
topics.description: This cluster centers on measuring cerebrovascular reactivity, cerebral blood flow, and related neurovascular dynamics using MRI and ASL-based approaches. The abstracts emphasize baseline physiology, resting-state and hypercapnia challenges, and methods for mapping regional vascular function.

topics.title: PET Imaging of Metabolic and Network Changes
topics.description: These abstracts center on PET-based measures of metabolism, microglial density, and network reorganization in brain disorders and pharmacologic challenge studies. They also include functional and structural connectivity findings involving the default mode network and altered perception.

topics.title: Transdiagnostic Neuroimaging in Psychosis
topics.description: This cluster centers on heterogeneity and transdiagnostic patterns across schizophrenia and psychosis spectrum disorders, linking symptom dimensions with structural and functional brain variation. It highlights data-driven neuroimaging signatures, cortical thickness alteration, and large-scale connectivity differences across psychiatric presentations.

keywords: support synchronous activity in human brain networks
topics.title: Brain Networks, Aging, and Molecular Signatures
topics.description: This cluster centers on how human brain network organization and structural connectome alterations relate to brain ageing and related neuropsychiatric conditions. It also links imaging features to gene expression, transcriptomic, and other molecular signatures, including cross-species and deep learning approaches for brain age estimation.

topics.title: Depression Heterogeneity and Brain Signatures
topics.description: This cluster centers on depressive disorders and related subtypes, with emphasis on adolescent and major depression. The abstracts highlight heterogeneous brain connectivity, cortical and white matter alterations, and molecular or transcriptional signatures associated with these conditions.

keywords: multiple demand network tracks cognitive reappraisal
topics.title: Neural and Autonomic Threat Learning in Anxiety
topics.description: This cluster centers on how fear, threat, and anxiety are represented and regulated in the brain and autonomic nervous system, especially during fear conditioning, extinction, and reappraisal. It also includes virtual reality and EEG/SEEG approaches used to probe threat processing and individual differences in anxiety-related responses.

topics.title: Emotion Regulation and Neural Correlates in Anxiety
topics.description: This cluster centers on emotion regulation processes, especially reappraisal, acceptance-based strategies, and social influences, in relation to anxiety and transdiagnostic psychopathology. It also highlights neural correlates involving the amygdala and distinctions between state and trait regulation, often across adolescent or large-scale study contexts.

topics.title: Neural Representations Across Human Vision
topics.description: This cluster centers on how human visual cortex representations relate to neural networks, naturalistic stimuli, and encoding/alignment analyses. It emphasizes shared neural codes, representational hierarchy, and whether model representations can predict or mirror brain activity and behavior.

keywords: midlife diet quality shapes late life brain health
topics.title: Vascular and Genetic Influences on Brain Aging
topics.description: This cluster centers on how vascular, metabolic, inflammatory, and genetic factors relate to brain structure and cognitive decline in aging. It includes associations with white matter integrity, cortical measures, dementia risk, and late-life brain health.

topics.title: Predictive Neural Processing in Natural Language
topics.description: This cluster centers on how the brain encodes and responds to naturalistic language, especially sentence and narrative comprehension. It also includes computational and neuroimaging approaches linking predictive coding, language models, and MEG/fMRI responses.

topics.title: Multimodal fMRI Decoding and Brain Representation
topics.description: This cluster centers on fMRI-based decoding and representation learning, especially multimodal and large-scale models for linking brain activity to meaning. It also includes work on cross-subject prediction, voxel selectivity, and methods that bridge temporal scales in human brain data.

topics.title: White Matter and Atrophy in Alzheimer Spectrum
topics.description: This cluster focuses on structural brain changes across the Alzheimer disease spectrum, especially white matter abnormalities and cortical or hippocampal atrophy. It also emphasizes early neuroimaging biomarkers and their diagnostic relevance in cognitively normal and clinical populations.

keywords: resting state fmri cerebrospinal fluid oscillations
cognitive task evoke brain body interactions
explore brainstem autonomic and cardiorespiratory signal interaction
cardiac pulsatility generates structured network specific bold dynamics
topics.title: Brain-body pulsatility and CSF coupling
topics.description: This cluster focuses on interactions between cardiac, cardiorespiratory, and brain pulsations, especially their coupling with BOLD fMRI and cerebrospinal fluid oscillations. It includes resting-state and task-evoked brain-body dynamics, with links to sleep and glymphatic-related physiology.

keywords: eeg resting state complexity and entropy
topics.title: Sleep Dynamics, Memory, and Brain Coupling
topics.description: This cluster centers on human sleep and its neural signatures, including NREM/REM states, spindles, K complexes, and dynamic functional connectivity. It also links sleep physiology to memory processing, glymphatic flow, and locus coeruleus-related brain coupling.

topics.title: EEG-Based Epilepsy Detection and Analysis
topics.description: This cluster centers on epilepsy research using EEG analysis, automated detection, and deep learning methods to identify epileptic patterns and related brain states. It also includes broader clinical and methodological work on outcomes, disorders, and evaluation frameworks.

keywords: connectome harmonics reveal alzheimer tau connectivity alignment predict cognitive decline
distributional tau neurodegeneration modeling reveal vulnerability resilience
topics.title: Tau spread and cognitive decline in Alzheimer disease
topics.description: This cluster centers on tau pathology, its progression and pathological spread, and how these processes relate to cognitive decline in Alzheimer disease. It also highlights vulnerability-resilience patterns, APOE4, blood biomarkers, and modeling approaches for predicting individual trajectories.

topics.title: Multimodal Alzheimer’s Subtyping and Prediction
topics.description: This cluster centers on multimodal approaches to identify Alzheimer’s and related neurodegenerative disease subtypes, track biomarker progression, and predict diagnosis or cognitive decline. It emphasizes amyloid/tau frameworks, subtype trajectories, and scalable or federated modeling for individualized risk assessment.

topics.title: Amyloid and Tau PET in Alzheimer Progression
topics.description: This cluster centers on Alzheimer disease across the AD continuum, with emphasis on amyloid and tau pathology measured by PET. It also includes related processes such as amyloid deposition, cortical burden, and mechanisms of tau accumulation and clearance.

keywords: multimodal neuroimaging case study of blindsense
topics.title: Visual Processing in Blindness and Hallucination
topics.description: This cluster centers on how visual cortex and related pathways reorganize in blindness, retinal degeneration, and blindsense, with attention to hallucinatory and intact perceptual representations. It also includes studies of topographic organization and visual filling-in phenomena such as contour integration and amodal completion.

topics.title: EEG Connectivity in Epilepsy
topics.description: This cluster centers on epilepsy research using EEG and intracranial electrophysiology to characterize seizure-related brain dynamics. It emphasizes connectivity, brain signal complexity, and localization of epileptogenic and seizure onset zones.

keywords: unsupervised modeling of eeg state space dynamics reveal latent neural profiles relate
topics.title: EEG Microstates and Brain Dynamics
topics.description: This cluster centers on EEG microstate analysis and related state-space or dynamical modeling approaches for characterizing brain dynamics. The studies link microstate and connectivity changes to network reorganization and latent neural profiles in disorders such as multiple sclerosis and psychosis.

keywords: densely sample single subject task fmri data
topics.title: Auditory Cortex Mapping in Developmental Disorders
topics.description: This cluster centers on auditory cortex organization and receptive field mapping, especially in developmental conditions such as autism and isolated auditory processing disorder. It also includes fMRI-based approaches and analyses of tonotopic and cortical dynamics in human auditory processing.

topics.title: Advanced MRI Methods for Brain Microstructure
topics.description: This cluster centers on advanced MRI approaches for probing brain microstructure, tissue composition, and temperature-related or relaxation-based contrasts. The abstracts emphasize high-resolution diffusion, CEST, magnetization transfer, and quantitative imaging methods across neuroscience applications.

keywords: inclusive unsedated brain mri protocol for neurodiverse children with behaviours of concern
topics.title: Paediatric MRI Training and Neurodiversity
topics.description: This cluster centers on inclusive, unsedated pediatric MRI approaches for neurodiverse children, with emphasis on reducing head motion and improving scan success. It also includes training, virtual reality, and child-friendly protocols used to support imaging in young children.

topics.title: Language Lateralization Across Scales
topics.description: This cluster centers on how language dominance and hemispheric asymmetry are organized across behavioral, structural, and functional levels. It also includes links to plasticity, neurochemical asymmetries, and clinical mapping approaches.

keywords: consistent scalable brain effective connectivity estimation
biophysical large scale brain circuit models
topics.title: Dynamic Brain Connectivity and State Modeling
topics.description: This cluster centers on modeling time-varying brain activity and connectivity from fMRI, including dynamic functional and effective connectivity, forecasting, and attractor-based dynamics. It also includes biologically constrained and biophysical brain models used to explain large-scale neural state transitions.

keywords: nonlinear large scale neural model inversion
topics.title: Personalized Brain Network Modeling for Neuromodulation
topics.description: This cluster centers on personalized and virtual brain models used to simulate large-scale neural dynamics, infer parameters, and predict responses to stimulation or disease states. A major emphasis is on brain network models and biophysical simulations for tailoring neuromodulation and understanding epileptic and thalamocortical mechanisms.

keywords: functional division of hippocampal long axis at encoding vs consolidation of threat relate memories
neural circuitry of spatial navigation in children with prenatal alcohol exposure
topics.title: Spatial Navigation and Memory Consolidation Networks
topics.description: This cluster centers on hippocampal and cortical mechanisms supporting spatial navigation, allocentric coding, and memory transformation across encoding and consolidation. It also includes work on prenatal alcohol exposure, network dynamics, and factors that predict memory durability.

topics.title: Neural Basis of Musical Expertise
topics.description: This cluster centers on how musical training and expertise relate to brain specialization, auditory processing, and neural encoding of music-related structure and performance. It also includes studies of music perception, pleasure, and predispositions using fMRI and twin designs.

topics.title: Neurofunctional and Molecular Imaging in Schizophrenia
topics.description: This cluster centers on schizophrenia and related psychiatric phenotypes, emphasizing functional alterations, symptom profiles, and individual variability. It also includes PET-based molecular imaging of neurotransmitter systems and receptors in the human brain.

keywords: neural networks of prefrontal and hippocampal contributions to conceptual change
rehearsal drives rapid transition in memory systems contributions
topics.title: Hippocampal and Prefrontal Learning Mechanisms
topics.description: This cluster centers on hippocampal and prefrontal contributions to learning, memory, and conceptual change. It links memory systems, relational integration, and rehearsal or spacing effects across science learning, implicit memory, and long-term memory.

topics.title: Hippocampal memory representation and navigation
topics.description: This cluster centers on hippocampal organization and its role in memory representation, pattern separation, and the encoding and retrieval of knowledge. It also includes work on navigation and representational structure across the hippocampal axis in human fMRI studies.

topics.title: Neural Bases of Language Comprehension
topics.description: This cluster centers on how the human brain supports language comprehension across modalities, including reading, sign language, and narrative or complex sentence processing. It also includes work on cortical representation, neural architecture, and individual differences, often using fMRI and dynamic causal modeling.

topics.title: Neural bases of speech and language perception
topics.description: This cluster centers on how speech and language are represented and processed in the brain, including perception, comprehension, production, and second-language experience. It also highlights neural correlates of pronunciation, speaker traits, and sensorimotor integration during language tasks.

topics.title: Brain Network Changes After Mild TBI
topics.description: This cluster centers on concussion and mild traumatic brain injury, especially in pediatric and sports-related cases. The abstracts emphasize altered cortical and structural-functional networks, network trajectories, and symptom markers linked to recovery delay and brain change.

keywords: deep learning models of acute stroke severity
improve prediction of post stroke language outcomes
early parietofrontal connectivity predicts visuomotor recovery after stroke
topics.title: Stroke Lesions and Outcome Prediction
topics.description: This cluster centers on how stroke lesions and disconnection patterns relate to language, cognition, and functional recovery. A major emphasis is on predicting acute severity and longer-term outcomes using network-based and machine learning approaches.

topics.title: Brain Connectome Models for Phenotype Prediction
topics.description: This cluster centers on machine learning and connectome-based approaches for predicting phenotypes, individual differences, and sex from functional and multimodal brain data. It includes foundation models, generative methods, and individualized parcellations aimed at improving brain-behavior prediction.

keywords: multimodal brain connectivity integration for cognitive performance prediction in youth
longitudinal multimodal neuroimaging fusion for predicting working memory and attention
multilevel brain information integration support consciousness state transitions
topics.title: Multimodal Brain Connectivity for Cognitive Prediction
topics.description: This cluster focuses on integrating multimodal neuroimaging and brain connectivity measures to predict cognitive outcomes such as working memory, attention, and broader cognitive performance in youth. It also includes related work on multilevel information integration and consciousness state transitions.

topics.title: Personalized Functional Brain Network Mapping
topics.description: This cluster centers on individualized and personalized functional network analysis in fMRI, including efforts to identify stable brain network fingerprints and characterize spatial topography. It also includes related template-based and fusion approaches for robust multimodal neuroimaging analysis.

keywords: ultra high b value diffusion mri
topics.title: Diffusion MRI Microstructure and Validation
topics.description: This cluster centers on advanced diffusion MRI approaches for estimating brain microstructure and complex morphology, often using high b-value data and novel modeling methods. It also includes validation against postmortem human brain tissue and related virtual histology frameworks.

keywords: ex vivo human brainstem mri ps oct
topics.title: Multimodal MRI and Tissue Imaging Biomarkers
topics.description: This cluster centers on advanced imaging approaches for characterizing human brain microstructure, including diffusion MRI, MRI signal processing, and ex vivo brainstem imaging. It also links tissue-level microscopy and transcriptomic integration toward quantitative biomarkers for neurodegenerative disease.

topics.title: Structural Connectivity in Pediatric Motor Disorders
topics.description: This cluster centers on how structural and functional connectivity relate to motor impairment and recovery in pediatric neurological conditions, especially after stroke and in cerebral palsy. It also includes studies using DTI and MEG to characterize white matter adaptation and motor pathway abnormalities.

topics.title: Dynamic Brain Connectivity in Neuropsychiatric Disorders
topics.description: This cluster centers on changes in brain network organization and functional connectivity, especially dynamic versus static patterns during resting-state fMRI. It is strongly oriented toward disease-related alterations in disorders such as Alzheimer disease, dementia, and schizophrenia.

topics.title: White Matter Microstructure Across the Lifespan
topics.description: This cluster centers on how white matter microstructure changes across development, adulthood, and aging, with emphasis on age-related differences and longitudinal lifespan effects. It also links white matter measures to neurodevelopmental and neurodegenerative conditions, including multiple sclerosis.

topics.title: Human Brain Circuit Mapping in Subcortical Networks
topics.description: This cluster focuses on mapping human brain circuits and white matter organization, with attention to subcortical structures, cortical connections, and tract subdivisions. It includes studies of tractography-based connectivity, microstructure, and circuit pathways relevant to brain organization and disease.

keywords: microstructural basis of structure function coupling across human resting state networks
topics.title: Thalamocortical Development and Connectivity
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how thalamic and thalamocortical connectivity develops across infancy and maturation, including structural covariance, functional gradients, and parcellation. It also highlights the microstructural basis of structure-function coupling in human resting state networks.

keywords: network level analysis toolbox for connectome wide association study
structural connectivity informed functional signal analysis
topics.title: Human Brain Connectome Dynamics and Communication
topics.description: This cluster centers on how structural and functional organization interact to shape communication, hierarchy, and long-range interactions in the human brain. It also includes connectome-based modeling and analytical tools used to study directed connectivity and network-level effects across brain systems.

topics.title: Personalized Brain Network Organization
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how individualized and connectome-level brain network properties relate to cognition, aging, and psychiatric symptoms. It emphasizes functional topology, network integration, and personalized resting-state connectivity patterns across diverse cohorts.

topics.title: Functional Connectome Fingerprinting Reliability
topics.description: This cluster centers on identifying individuals from resting-state fMRI and evaluating how reliable functional connectivity measures are across scans, cohorts, and preprocessing choices. It emphasizes fingerprinting performance, reproducibility, and factors such as scan length, denoising, and censored data.

keywords: human brain dynamics change across the lifespan
spatiotemporal pattern of individual functional variability in youth
topics.title: Lifespan Brain Dynamics and Functional Variability
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how brain functional organization changes across development and aging, with attention to youth, neonates, and typical aging. It emphasizes individual variability, connectivity subtypes, asymmetry, and altered network interactions in relation to cognition.

keywords: progressive multi stage framework for longitudinal infant brain network completion
topics.title: Neonatal Brain Network Development
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how infant and neonatal brain networks reorganize across early development, including functional connectivity, gradients, and maturation trajectories. It also examines how preterm birth and developmental timing relate to later cognitive outcomes.

topics.title: Preterm Infant EEG and Brain Development
topics.description: This cluster focuses on EEG and MRI markers of brain development in preterm and term infants, with attention to connectivity, spectral features, and regional patterns. It also links early neural measures to sleep quality, vigilance states, and later developmental outcomes.
